days.title: Inverse Captcha
days.essence: Sum digits that match the next digit around a circular sequence; part 2 just changes the comparison offset to halfway around. Pure warmup to learn the I/O format.

days.title: Corruption Checksum
days.essence: Compute a checksum from each row's min/max difference; part 2 finds the unique evenly-divisible pair per row. Straightforward spreadsheet iteration with no real algorithmic depth.

days.title: Spiral Memory
days.essence: Map an index on an outward (Ulam) spiral to coordinates and return its Manhattan distance, which the solver derives via a closed-form ring formula. Part 2 switches to summing already-filled neighbors until a value exceeds the input, a small grid-growth twist.

days.title: High-Entropy Passphrases
days.essence: Count passphrases with no duplicate words; part 2 forbids anagrams by sorting each word's letters before deduping. Simple set-based string validation.

days.title: A Maze of Twisty Trampolines, All Alike
days.essence: Follow jump offsets through a list, mutating each offset as you leave it, and count steps to escape. Part 2 tweaks the mutation rule (decrement when the offset is large), still just literal step-by-step simulation.

days.title: Memory Reallocation
days.essence: Repeatedly redistribute the fullest bank's blocks until a configuration repeats; the answer is when (and how long ago) the loop closes. The core idea is hashing seen states to detect the cycle, which part 2 reuses for the loop length.

days.title: Recursive Circus
days.essence: Build a weighted tree from parent-to-children lines and find its root; part 2 recurses through cumulative subtree weights to find the single mis-weighted node and its corrected value. The difficulty is the recursive balance reasoning in part 2.

days.title: I Heard You Like Registers
days.essence: Interpret conditional inc/dec instructions over named registers and report the largest final value; part 2 just tracks the all-time peak. A small register-machine interpreter.

days.title: Stream Processing
days.essence: Scan a character stream as a state machine handling nested groups, garbage, and ! escapes, scoring group nesting depth. Part 2 counts garbage characters, reusing the same single pass.

days.title: Knot Hash
days.essence: Implement the custom Knot Hash: repeatedly reverse circular spans advancing a skipped pointer. Part 2 layers on ASCII length suffixes, 64 rounds, and an XOR-folded dense hash to produce the hex digest used by later days.

days.title: Hex Ed
days.essence: Walk a path of hex-grid steps and compute distance back to the origin using axial/cube coordinates. Part 2 tracks the maximum distance ever reached along the walk.

days.title: Digital Plumber
days.essence: Find the size of the connected component containing program 0 via BFS; part 2 counts all connected components. A plain connectivity / union-of-groups exercise.

days.title: Packet Scanners
days.essence: Each scanner has a ping-pong period; severity is the sum over layers caught at time = depth. Part 2 finds the smallest start delay that is never caught, a modular-residue search over all layers.

days.title: Disk Defragmentation
days.essence: Generate a 128x128 bit grid from 128 Knot Hashes and count used squares; part 2 counts connected regions of used squares via flood fill. Combines the day-10 hash with grid component counting.

days.title: Dueling Generators
days.essence: Run two multiplicative generators for tens of millions of steps and count when their low 16 bits match. Part 2 only judges values divisible by 4 and 8, so the real work is fast generation and bitmask comparison at scale.

days.title: Permutation Promenade
days.essence: Apply a dance of spin/exchange/partner moves to a row of programs. Part 2 asks for one billion rounds, which is only tractable by detecting the repeating cycle and jumping to the equivalent remainder.

days.title: Spinlock
days.essence: Insert values into a circular buffer stepping forward each time, then read the value after the last insert. Part 2 needs 50 million inserts, so you stop materializing the buffer and just track whatever lands right after index 0.

days.title: Duet
days.essence: Implement a register machine with snd/rcv where part 1 recovers the last sound played. Part 2 (unsolved) reinterprets snd/rcv as two concurrent programs exchanging messages, requiring interleaved execution and deadlock detection to count sends.

days.title: A Series of Tubes
days.essence: Follow a path drawn in an ASCII grid, turning only at + corners, collecting the letters passed. Part 2 simply counts the total steps walked along the same traversal.

days.title: Particle Swarm
days.essence: Integrate position/velocity/acceleration of 3D particles to find the one that stays nearest the origin long-term (smallest acceleration). Part 2 removes particles that share a position each tick; the solver simulates enough rounds for collisions to settle rather than solving collisions analytically.

days.title: Fractal Art
days.essence: Repeatedly split a pixel grid into 2x2/3x3 blocks and replace each via rotation/flip-matched enhancement rules, counting lit pixels. Part 1 runs 5 iterations, but part 2's 18 iterations explode the grid, so the trick is caching how each block evolves rather than tracking the whole image.

days.title: Sporifica Virus
days.essence: A carrier turns based on the current node's state, toggles it, and moves on an infinite grid, counting infections over 10,000 bursts. Part 2 adds weakened/flagged intermediate states and 10 million bursts, demanding an efficient sparse-grid simulation.

days.title: Coprocessor Conflagration
days.essence: Part 1 emulates a set/sub/mul/jnz machine and counts mul executions. Part 2 cannot be brute-forced; you must disassemble your specific program to see it is counting composite numbers in a range and replace the inner loop with a primality check.

days.title: Electromagnetic Moat
days.essence: Build the strongest port-matching bridge from components, starting at port 0 and using each component once. Part 2 instead maximizes strength among the longest bridges; both are exhaustive DFS over all assemblable chains with pruning.

days.title: The Halting Problem
days.essence: Parse a Turing-machine blueprint and run it for a given number of steps on an infinite tape, then count the 1s for the diagnostic checksum. The work is parsing the state table and faithfully simulating the read/write/move/transition rules.
